lesson.menu_title: Block 2 Review -- Leading a Supplier Meeting
lesson.menu_desc: Put it all together -- chair a full supplier meeting from open to close with the chair's words and all the Block 2 language -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Block 2 Review -- Leading a Supplier Meeting -- chairing a full meeting with integrated meeting language
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Andreia Heins | Aula 20 -- Block 2 Review: Leading a Supplier Meeting

listenings.text: Hi Andreia, David Lewis here. You are chairing the supplier meeting today, so let me brief you on how to run it well. Start by welcoming everyone and running through the agenda, so the whole team is on the same page from the first minute. Keep it on time. If one point takes too long, do not let it run away from you. Table it for a later meeting and move to the next item. When you discuss the budget, weigh the options out loud: if we ordered two consoles, we would save time, but the cost is higher. Invite people to take the floor, and when it is time to decide, take a quick show of hands. If everyone agrees, sign off on the plan. Before you close, sum up the main takeaway and agree the next steps, with one clear owner and a date for each. Then thank everyone and adjourn. You have all the language for this. Good luck, Andreia. Talk soon.

All right, everyone, let me close the meeting. Thank you all for your time and your input today. Let me sum up. The main takeaway is clear: we approved the second console, and we are all on the same page on the budget. Here are the next steps. First, I will sign off on the plan and send the summary by tomorrow. Second, the supplier will confirm the earlier training date by Friday. Third, we tabled the warranty discussion, so I will add it to the agenda for next month. Could you each review your own action item and reply if anything is unclear? I would like one owner and one date for every step. We finished on time and we made a real decision, so thank you for that. There is nothing else on the agenda, so let us adjourn. Have a great day, and I will see you at the next meeting.

extra_audio.text: Let me describe how Andreia chairs the meeting. First, she opens and runs through the agenda so everyone is on the same page. Then she invites people to take the floor and discusses the budget with the second conditional. Next, she takes a show of hands and signs off on the plan. After that, she tables the item there is no time for. Finally, she sums up the takeaway, agrees the next steps, and adjourns the meeting.
